symptoms.symptom_name: Immediate 'pop' after a fall onto an outstretched hand

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Classic mechanism and audible/felt pop are typical of glenohumeral dislocation.
Same mechanism can fracture the proximal humerus — cannot be distinguished on history alone.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absolute inability to move the joint; arm locked in slight abduction/external rotation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A limb fixed in abduction/external rotation with total loss of movement is characteristic of anterior dislocation.
AC separation preserves shoulder movement — it does not lock the arm out.

symptoms.symptom_name: Gross deformity — squared-off contour and anterior bony prominence; palpable empty glenoid beneath the acromion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A palpable empty glenoid with an anteriorly displaced head and squared-off deltoid is close to pathognomonic; the visible squared-off contour is video-observable but true palpation of the empty glenoid requires an examiner.

symptoms.symptom_name: Numbness/tingling over the lateral upper arm (axillary nerve distribution)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Lateral deltoid sensory loss with a shoulder dislocation indicates axillary nerve traction — the commonest neurological complication.
Axillary nerve involvement is a recognised association of anterior dislocation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Distal circulation and sensation — intact radial pulse, capillary refill <2 s, hand warm/pink with intact finger movement

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A warm, pink, well-perfused, moving hand with a present pulse excludes current distal ischaemia — a patient/carer can observe colour and movement, but a true pulse/cap-refill exam requires an examiner; loss of any of these flips the tier to T5.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain-driven vital signs — HR 110, BP 140/85, RR 20, SpO2 99%, afebrile

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild tachycardia and hypertension reflect severe pain/distress; haemodynamically stable and not a marker of shock — clinician-measured, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Plain shoulder radiograph — loss of articular congruity with anterior humeral-head displacement; excludes/confirms fracture

investigation_details.investigation_name: Plain shoulder radiograph (AP + axillary or scapular-Y views)
investigation_details.expected_result: Anterior/antero-inferior displacement of the humeral head with loss of glenohumeral congruity; assess for concomitant proximal humerus or glenoid fracture
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: An unrecognised fracture-dislocation may be reduced blindly, shattering the humerus.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Radiographic loss of congruity with anterior head displacement is the confirmatory finding and the mandatory pre-reduction step — never available in telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Gross joint deformity + inability to move the joint + palpable empty joint space + radiographic loss of articular congruity form the classic diagnostic tetrad of anterior glenohumeral dislocation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Plain shoulder radiograph (AP + axillary or scapular-Y views) BEFORE reduction
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the dislocation and its direction and, critically, excludes a concomitant fracture-dislocation before any reduction is attempted.
Confirms successful relocation and detects any fracture caused or revealed by reduction.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Documented neurovascular assessment (axillary nerve sensation, distal pulses) pre- and post-reduction
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Baselines the axillary neuropraxia and detects any procedure-related deterioration.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: CT/MRI of the shoulder in the acute setting
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not required acutely for an uncomplicated dislocation; reserved for complex fracture-dislocations or later assessment of soft-tissue/labral injury.
